roles.title: Review Program Owner
roles.responsibility: Own scope, sequencing, deviations, records, activation requests, and stop decisions.
minimum_capability: Understand Ming Foundation review boundaries
Maintain version, scope, decision, and deviation records
Recognize when work must stop or escalate
prohibited_combinations: May not be the sole independent facilitator for CP3
May not be the sole approver of a complaint concerning their own decision

roles.title: Safeguarding Owner
roles.responsibility: Own distress, disclosure, safeguarding, emergency-boundary, and stop escalation.
minimum_capability: Relevant child, adolescent, family, mental-health, safeguarding, or duty-of-care competence
Ability to distinguish review from clinical or emergency service
Ability to activate stop, referral, and independent review routes
prohibited_combinations: May not close a concern in which they may be the source of harm
May not be the sole reviewer of their own safeguarding action

roles.title: Evidence Custodian
roles.responsibility: Own restricted evidence access, retention, deletion, export, restoration, and audit.
minimum_capability: Operate access-controlled storage
Maintain deletion, restoration, export, and access evidence
Separate participant contact data from review evidence
prohibited_combinations: May not be the sole privacy approver for an incident involving their own access action
May not authorize unrelated reuse of review evidence

roles.title: Accessibility and Language Owner
roles.responsibility: Own communication access, interpretation, disability support, and accommodations.
minimum_capability: Assess whether rights are usable through the offered channel
Coordinate language and accessibility support
Record unresolved access barriers without blaming the participant
prohibited_combinations: May not infer consent or capacity from inability to use an inaccessible channel

roles.title: Independent Facilitator Lead
roles.responsibility: Own conflict-free facilitation for child, adolescent, vulnerable-person, and contested tracks.
minimum_capability: Facilitate without leading or coercing
Recognize distress, silence, refusal, and power imbalance
Preserve minority and dissenting views
prohibited_combinations: May not be the participant's coercive supervisor, evaluator, or disputed representative
May not be the sole program owner for CP3

roles.title: Jurisdiction and Professional Review Owner
roles.responsibility: Own operating-context, legal, regulatory, safeguarding, professional, and local qualification.
minimum_capability: Qualified for the stated jurisdiction or able to document reliance on a qualified reviewer
Identify age, consent, representation, retention, disclosure, and professional-duty limits
State uncertainty and prohibited contexts
prohibited_combinations: May not approve outside documented competence or jurisdiction

roles.title: Recruitment and Compensation Owner
roles.responsibility: Own non-coercive recruitment, compensation, contact, eligibility, and conflict boundaries.
minimum_capability: Separate service access from research or review participation
Avoid dependency, authority, payment, and family-pressure coercion
Maintain contact-data separation
prohibited_combinations: May not be the sole consent approver for people they directly supervise or serve

roles.title: Privacy and Data-Rights Reviewer
roles.responsibility: Independently review collection, notice, consent, withdrawal, correction, deletion, and reuse.
minimum_capability: Review minimization, purpose limitation, access, retention, deletion, and rights routes
Challenge evidence collection that is not necessary
Require correction and deletion propagation
prohibited_combinations: May not be the sole reviewer of a privacy incident caused by their own action
May not approve unrelated reuse of evidence they control
